entry.title: REST vs gRPC - Under the Hood
entry.summary: Resource APIs over HTTP/JSON versus function calls over HTTP/2 and protobuf. Stack placement, streaming, and when enterprises use both.
entry.content: Resource APIs over HTTP/JSON versus function calls over HTTP/2 and protobuf. Stack placement, streaming, and when enterprises use both.

entry.title: HTTP/1.1 vs HTTP/2 vs HTTP/3 - Under the Hood
entry.summary: One HTTP application model, three wire contracts: text + TCP, multiplexed binary on TLS/TCP, and HTTP over QUIC on UDP. Head-of-line blocking, streams, and when each wins.
entry.content: One HTTP application model, three wire contracts: text + TCP, multiplexed binary on TLS/TCP, and HTTP over QUIC on UDP. Head-of-line blocking, streams, and when each wins.

entry.title: Load Balancer - Under the Hood
entry.summary: Layer 4 vs Layer 7 load balancing: what each sees on the wire, network vs application load balancers, algorithms, health checks, and when to use which.
entry.content: Layer 4 vs Layer 7 load balancing: what each sees on the wire, network vs application load balancers, algorithms, health checks, and when to use which.

entry.title: SQL vs NoSQL - Under the Hood
entry.summary: Relational tables, document stores, key-value, graph, vector, and time-series: what each model is, when to use it, when not to, and the tradeoffs under the hood.
entry.content: Relational tables, document stores, key-value, graph, vector, and time-series: what each model is, when to use it, when not to, and the tradeoffs under the hood.

entry.title: LLM Hosting Options for Regulated Industries
entry.summary: From public SaaS to air-gapped on-prem: a practical ladder of LLM hosting options, and how to choose based on regulatory pressure and engineering maturity.
entry.content: From public SaaS to air-gapped on-prem: a practical ladder of LLM hosting options, and how to choose based on regulatory pressure and engineering maturity.

entry.title: HTTPS Encryption Lifecycle - Under the Hood
entry.summary: One HTTPS call from TCP open through TLS handshake, session keys, AEAD records, and teardown. Where asymmetric trust ends and symmetric bulk encryption begins.
entry.content: One HTTPS call from TCP open through TLS handshake, session keys, AEAD records, and teardown. Where asymmetric trust ends and symmetric bulk encryption begins.

entry.title: HTTP vs HTTPS - Under the Hood
entry.summary: HTTP is the application protocol for requests and responses. HTTPS is HTTP over TLS. Methods, status, headers, the TLS wrap, certificates, and which failures people mislabel as network or app.
entry.content: HTTP is the application protocol for requests and responses. HTTPS is HTTP over TLS. Methods, status, headers, the TLS wrap, certificates, and which failures people mislabel as network or app.

entry.title: Symmetric vs Asymmetric Encryption - Under the Hood
entry.summary: Symmetric crypto shares one secret for speed. Asymmetric uses key pairs for trust and key exchange. Where each appears in TLS, APIs, data at rest, and which mistakes look like security.
entry.content: Symmetric crypto shares one secret for speed. Asymmetric uses key pairs for trust and key exchange. Where each appears in TLS, APIs, data at rest, and which mistakes look like security.

entry.title: CDN - Under the Hood
entry.summary: A CDN is a cache-and-route fabric at the edge: PoPs, origin, cache keys, TTL, anycast, and the hit/miss path. Why delivery scales, and which failures people mislabel as app or network.
entry.content: A CDN is a cache-and-route fabric at the edge: PoPs, origin, cache keys, TTL, anycast, and the hit/miss path. Why delivery scales, and which failures people mislabel as app or network.

entry.title: TCP vs UDP - Under the Hood
entry.summary: TCP is a reliable byte stream with handshake, ACKs, and congestion control. UDP is datagrams with no delivery guarantee. Components, steps, use cases, and which failures people mislabel as app bugs.
entry.content: TCP is a reliable byte stream with handshake, ACKs, and congestion control. UDP is datagrams with no delivery guarantee. Components, steps, use cases, and which failures people mislabel as app bugs.

entry.title: Anycast vs Unicast - Under the Hood
entry.summary: Unicast maps one IP to one place. Anycast maps one IP to many places and lets BGP pick the path. How both work, where DNS and CDNs use them, and which failures look like app bugs.
entry.content: Unicast maps one IP to one place. Anycast maps one IP to many places and lets BGP pick the path. How both work, where DNS and CDNs use them, and which failures look like app bugs.

entry.title: DNS - Under the Hood
entry.summary: DNS is a globally distributed, hierarchical, eventually consistent naming database. Stub, recursive resolver, root, TLD, authoritative name server, caching, TTL, and anycast.
entry.content: DNS is a globally distributed, hierarchical, eventually consistent naming database. Stub, recursive resolver, root, TLD, authoritative name server, caching, TTL, and anycast.

entry.title: The Blueprint Before Training an LLM
entry.summary: Hyperparameters, vocabulary, and why a 7B model is 7B before day one, how the dials connect, where parameters live, and why the context window is a budgeted choice.
entry.content: Hyperparameters, vocabulary, and why a 7B model is 7B before day one, how the dials connect, where parameters live, and why the context window is a budgeted choice.

entry.title: Is MCP Really Necessary for Business Agents in Large Regulated Enterprises?
entry.summary: MCP is a valuable standard for AI tool interoperability. For regulated business agents, the question is whether its benefits outweigh operational cost for your use case, not whether MCP is good.
entry.content: MCP is a valuable standard for AI tool interoperability. For regulated business agents, the question is whether its benefits outweigh operational cost for your use case, not whether MCP is good.

entry.title: Retrieval Is a Governed Action
entry.summary: Retrieval is not a database query; it is a governed action. How Policy-Governed Agent Runtime applies to RAG when context construction must be scoped, auditable, and enforced before inference.
entry.content: Retrieval is not a database query; it is a governed action. How Policy-Governed Agent Runtime applies to RAG when context construction must be scoped, auditable, and enforced before inference.

entry.title: How to Design an Intent Router for Agentic AI
entry.summary: A practical design guide for intent routing — route tables, layered classification, confidence thresholds, session stickiness, eval gates, and wiring dispatch into the agentic app before the model loop runs.
entry.content: A practical design guide for intent routing — route tables, layered classification, confidence thresholds, session stickiness, eval gates, and wiring dispatch into the agentic app before the model loop runs.

entry.title: What Is an Intent Router — and Why It Matters in Agentic AI
entry.summary: An intent router is the first deterministic decision in an agent stack. It maps user requests to the right workflow, agent, and tool manifest before the model loop runs — and when it fails, every downstream stage can execute perfectly and still miss the user’s goal.
entry.content: An intent router is the first deterministic decision in an agent stack. It maps user requests to the right workflow, agent, and tool manifest before the model loop runs — and when it fails, every downstream stage can execute perfectly and still miss the user’s goal.

entry.title: RAG Is Not a Database
entry.summary: RAG is runtime context construction at query time, not a storage layer you bolt onto an LLM.
entry.content: RAG is runtime context construction at query time, not a storage layer you bolt onto an LLM.

entry.title: Policy-Governed Agent Runtime
entry.summary: Proposal is not permission. Agents propose tool calls; governance decides whether they run. An architecture breakdown of runtime trust boundaries for production agent systems in regulated industries.
entry.content: Proposal is not permission. Agents propose tool calls; governance decides whether they run. An architecture breakdown of runtime trust boundaries for production agent systems in regulated industries.

entry.title: AI Observability In Enterprise
entry.summary: AI observability is not a dashboard. It is a capture-and-retention architecture with five signals, five retention policies, and four consumers.
entry.content: AI observability is not a dashboard. It is a capture-and-retention architecture with five signals, five retention policies, and four consumers.
